channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Roblox debuts new child protection, parental control features
item.description: Following recent questions about child safety issues on the platform, Roblox announced Monday a number of changes designed to protect young users and increase parental control over the content they see.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: I'm so excited for Beyoncé's live NFL show on Netflix, despite the Tyson vs Paul streaming disaster
item.description: Netflix has announced Beyoncé will perform live at its Ravens-Houston match, where she's expected to sing songs from her latest album Cowboy Carter for the first time.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: AI platforms driving business transformation in the UAE: insights from industry experts
item.description: In today’s fast-paced digital landscape, AI platforms are playing a pivotal role in reshaping industries and driving business transformation.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Kyndryl, Microsoft expand mainframe modernization services
item.description: IT infrastructure services provider Kyndryl continues to build on its partnership with Microsoft, most recently adding new mainframe modernization support, security services, and cloud migration offerings.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New Chinese humanoid robot shows off its strength by lifting 35 pounds per hand
item.description: China's Shanghai Kepler Robotics has developed a new humanoid robot that can carry up to 35 pounds per hand for commercial applications across various industries
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple’s AirPods Pro 2 makes hearing tests as easy as a few taps
item.description: Checking your hearing has gotten easier with the new hearing test feature in AirPods Pro 2. You can take reliable hearing tests using your iPhone or iPad.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New framework aims to keep AI safe in US critical infrastructure
item.description: Analyst reaction to Thursday’s release by the US Department of Homeland Security (DHS) of a framework designed to ensure safe and secure deployment of AI in critical infrastructure is decidedly mixed.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: A challenge to recreate how the International Space Station was built
item.description: A new challenge looks to digitally recreate the planning of each module of the International Space Station. The post A challenge to recreate how the International Space Station was built first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: AI, cybersecurity drive IT investments and lead skill shortages for 2025
item.description: AI and cybersecurity are driving enterprise tech investments and at the same time exposing the talent gaps that IT teams face.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The enterprise service revolution: Supercharging ESM with AI
item.description: IT service management (ITSM) tools and practices transformed the tech side of the enterprise by automating service and support operations, along with adding employee self-service options.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: People are panic buying Plan B after Trump’s win—and Amazon sellers are raising the pills’ price
item.description: In the hours after Donald Trump was declared the winner of the 2024 presidential election, a number of people took to social media to hammer home a simple directive: Buy Plan B now, before it’s too late.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Blue Origin and SERA are creating ‘a space agency for everyone’ with a reality-style competition and TV series
item.description: When the fledgling Space Exploration and Research Agency (SERA) sent Victor Correa Hespanha into suborbital space with Blue Origin’s NS-21 mission two years ago, it hoped to spark more interest in STEM education in a country not known for its space industry.
item.dc:creator: Susan Karlin, Fast Company

item.title: Getting started with Google Password Manager
item.description: If you’re still trying to remember all of your passwords and then type ’em into sites by hand, let me tell you: You’re doing it wrong.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: In the wake of Java Development Kit (JDK) 23, which arrived September 17, work now focuses on the planned successor release, JDK 24, which has ballooned to 21 proposed features.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cruise admits to filing false report to sway federal probe into San Francisco crash
item.description: The autonomous car company did not include details about a woman being dragged over 20 feet by one of its self-driving vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Jason Green, The Mercury News

item.title: Polymarket at center of political storm after reported FBI raid
item.description: Prediction betting market Polymarket and its founder and CEO Shayne Coplan have found themselves at the center of a debate about political betting after the FBI reportedly raided Coplan’s home on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Musk: DOGE will be 'tedious work' with 'zero' compensation
item.description: Elon Musk said in a post on the social platform X on Thursday that the brand new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) — an advisory group under President-elect Trump focused on slashing government costs and restructuring federal agencies — will require "tedious work" with "zero" compensation.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Cloud creates network blind spots, complicates problem resolution
item.description: As network environments become more complex, network teams struggle to gain visibility across internet and cloud components, citing blind spots and a lack of skilled workers as contributing factors to slow problem resolution, according to a Broadcom report.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: One Tech Tip: Replacing passwords with passkeys for an easier login experience
item.description: LONDON (AP) — If you're tired of memorizing passwords, then give passkeys a try. You might have noticed that many online services are now offering the option of using passkeys, a digital authentication method touted as an easier and more secure way to log in.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk's bold plans for 'Department of Government Efficiency' face roadblocks
item.description: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy are set to take the reins of the brand new "Department of Government Efficiency" (DOGE) under President-elect Trump — a panel with bold ambitions of slashing government costs and restructuring federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: VMware makes Workstation and Fusion free for everyone
item.description: VMware this week announced that the virtualization company’s hypervisor products, VMware Workstation and Fusion, will be completely free for both personal and commercial users, as well as for educational purposes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Chinese hackers target Tibetan websites in malware attack, cybersecurity group says
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A hacking group that is believed to be Chinese state-sponsored has compromised two websites with ties to the Tibetan community in an attack meant to install malware on users' computers, according to findings released Wednesday by a private cybersecurity firm.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bitcoin neared $90,000 in a new record high. What to know about crypto's post-election rally
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As money continues to pour into crypto following Donald Trump's reelection last week, bitcoin has climbed to yet another record high. The world's largest cryptocurrency topped $89,000 for the first time, briefly peaking at $89,995 early Tuesday, according to CoinDesk.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Go language rises in Tiobe popularity index
item.description: The Google-built Go language has reached an all time high position in the Tiobe index of programming language popularity, ranking seventh in the November 2024 edition of the index. While Go already had cracked Tiobe’s top 10, the language had never ranked higher than eighth.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Facebook and Instagram users in Europe can opt for less personalized ads
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Facebook and Instagram users in Europe will get the option to see less personalized ads if they don't want to pay for an ad-free subscription, social media company Meta said Tuesday, bowing to pressure from Brussels over privacy and digital competition concerns.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Servicemember comes full-circle: Serving our nation as an Army sergeant, VA training specialist on the Digital GI Bill
item.description: Together, we can make sure that as veterans transition to civilian life, it opens doors to new opportunities and ways to thrive. The post Servicemember comes full-circle: Serving our nation as an Army sergeant, VA training specialist on the Digital GI Bill first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Japan's SoftBank returns to profit after gains at Vision Fund and other investments
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology group SoftBank swung back to profitability in the July-September quarter, boosted by positive results in its Vision Fund investments. Tokyo-based SoftBank Group Corp.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, NewsNation
